31: 32: 33: 34: 35: --- 36: 37: Chatwoot is the modern, open-source, and self-hosted customer support platform designed to help businesses deliver exceptional customer support experience. Built for scale and flexibility, Chatwoot gives you full control over your customer data while providing powerful tools to manage conversations across channels. 38: 39: ### ✨ Captain – AI Agent for Support 40: 41: Supercharge your support with Captain, Chatwoot’s AI agent. Captain helps automate responses, handle common queries, and reduce agent workload—ensuring customers get instant, accurate answers. With Captain, your team can focus on complex conversations while routine questions are resolved automatically. Read more about Captain [here](https://chwt.app/captain-docs). 42: 43: ### 💬 Omnichannel Support Desk 44: 45: Chatwoot centralizes all customer conversations into one powerful inbox, no matter where your customers reach out from. It supports live chat on your website, email, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, WhatsApp, Telegram, Line, SMS etc. 46: 47: ### 📚 Help center portal 48: 49: Publish help articles, FAQs, and guides through the built-in Help Center Portal. Enable customers to find answers on their own, reduce repetitive queries, and keep your support team focused on more complex issues. 50: 51: ### 🗂️ Other features 52: 53: #### Collaboration & Productivity 54: 55: - Private Notes and @mentions for internal team discussions. 56: - Labels to organize and categorize conversations. 57: - Keyboard Shortcuts and a Command Bar for quick navigation. 58: - Canned Responses to reply faster to frequently asked questions. 59: - Auto-Assignment to route conversations based on agent availability. 60: - Multi-lingual Support to serve customers in multiple languages. 61: - Custom Views and Filters for better inbox organization. 62: - Business Hours and Auto-Responders to manage response expectations. 63: - Teams and Automation tools for scaling support workflows. 64: - Agent Capacity Management to balance workload across the team. 65: 66: #### Customer Data & Segmentation 67: - Contact Management with profiles and interaction history. 68: - Contact Segments and Notes for targeted communication. 69: - Campaigns to proactively engage customers. 70: - Custom Attributes for storing additional customer data. 71: - Pre-Chat Forms to collect user information before starting conversations. 72: 73: #### Integrations 74: - Slack Integration to manage conversations directly from Slack. 75: - Dialogflow Integration for chatbot automation. 76: - Dashboard Apps to embed internal tools within Chatwoot. 77: - Shopify Integration to view and manage customer orders right within Chatwoot. 78: - Use Google Translate to translate messages from your customers in realtime. 79: - Create and manage Linear tickets within Chatwoot. 80: 81: #### Reports & Insights 82: - Live View of ongoing conversations for real-time monitoring. 83: - Conversation, Agent, Inbox, Label, and Team Reports for operational visibility. 84: - CSAT Reports to measure customer satisfaction. 85: - Downloadable Reports for offline analysis and reporting. 86: 87: 88: ## Documentation 89: 90: Detailed documentation is available at [chatwoot.com/help-center](https://www.chatwoot.com/help-center). 91: 92: ## Translation process 93: 94: The translation process for Chatwoot web and mobile app is managed at [https://translate.chatwoot.com](https://translate.chatwoot.com) using Crowdin. Please read the [translation guide](https://www.chatwoot.com/docs/contributing/translating-chatwoot-to-your-language) for contributing to Chatwoot. 95: 96: ## Branching model 97: 98: We use the [git-flow](https://nvie.com/posts/a-successful-git-branching-model/) branching model. The base branch is `develop`. 99: If you are looking for a stable version, please use the `master` or tags labelled as `v1.x.x`. 100: 101: ## Deployment 102: 103: ### Heroku one-click deploy 104: 105: Deploying Chatwoot to Heroku is a breeze. It's as simple as clicking this button: 106: 107: [![Deploy](https://www.herokucdn.com/deploy/button.svg)](https://heroku.com/deploy?template=https://github.com/chatwoot/chatwoot/tree/master) 108: 109: Follow this [link](https://www.chatwoot.com/docs/environment-variables) to understand setting the correct environment variables for the app to work with all the features. There might be breakages if you do not set the relevant environment variables. 110: 111: 112: ### DigitalOcean 1-Click Kubernetes deployment 113: 114: Chatwoot now supports 1-Click deployment to DigitalOcean as a kubernetes app. 115: 116: 117: 118: 119: 120: ### Other deployment options 121: 122: For other supported options, checkout our [deployment page](https://chatwoot.com/deploy). 123: 124: ## Security 125: 126: Looking to report a vulnerability? Please refer our [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) file. 127: 128: ## Community 129: 130: If you need help or just want to hang out, come, say hi on our [Discord](https://discord.gg/cJXdrwS) server. 131: 132: ## Contributors 133: 134: Thanks goes to all these [wonderful people](https://www.chatwoot.com/docs/contributors): 135: 136: 137: 138: 139: *Chatwoot* © 2017-2026, Chatwoot Inc - Released under the MIT License. 1: class DeleteObjectJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :low
3: 
4:   BATCH_SIZE = 5_000
5: 
6:   def perform(object, user = nil, ip = nil)
7:     # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid
8:     # timeouts & race conditions due to destroy_async fan-out.
9:     purge_heavy_associations(object)
10:     object.destroy!
11:     process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip)
12:   end
13: 
14:   def process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip); end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def heavy_associations
19:     {
20:       Account => %i[conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events],
21:       Inbox => %i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]
22:     }.freeze
23:   end
24: 
25:   def purge_heavy_associations(object)
26:     klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
27:     return unless klass
28: 
29:     heavy_associations[klass].each do |assoc|
30:       next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)
31: 
32:       batch_destroy(object.public_send(assoc))
33:     end
34:   end
35: 
36:   def batch_destroy(relation)
37:     relation.find_in_batches(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
38:       batch.each(&:destroy!)
39:     end
40:   end
41: end
42:

81:     case inbox.inbox_type
82:     when 'Twilio SMS'
83:       Twilio::OneoffSmsCampaignService.new(campaign: self).perform
84:     when 'Sms'
85:       Sms::OneoffSmsCampaignService.new(campaign: self).perform
86:     when 'Whatsapp'
87:       Whatsapp::OneoffCampaignService.new(campaign: self).perform

1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
5: 
6:   # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
7:   # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
8:   # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
9:   def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
10:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
11:     token = SecureRandom.uuid
12:     return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
13: 
14:     return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
15: 
16:     # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
17:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
18:     false
19:   rescue StandardError
20:     # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
21:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
22:     raise
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
26:     inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
27:     return unless inbox
28: 
29:     service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
30:     assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
31:     Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
32:   rescue StandardError => e
33:     Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
34:     raise e if Rails.env.test?
35:   ensure
36:     release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
37:   end
38: 
39:   private
40: 
41:   # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
42:   # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
43:   # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
44:   def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
45:     return if token.nil?
46: 
47:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
48:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
49:   end
50: 
51:   def bulk_assignment_limit
52:     ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
53:   end
54: end

1: # This class creates both outgoing messages from chatwoot and echo outgoing messages based on the flag `outgoing_echo`
2: # Assumptions
3: # 1. Incase of an outgoing message which is echo, source_id will NOT be nil,
4: #    based on this we are showing "not sent from chatwoot" message in frontend
5: #    Hence there is no need to set user_id in message for outgoing echo messages.
6: 
7: class Messages::Facebook::MessageBuilder < Messages::Messenger::MessageBuilder
8:   attr_reader :response
9: 
10:   def initialize(response, inbox, outgoing_echo: false)
11:     super()
12:     @response = response
13:     @inbox = inbox
14:     @outgoing_echo = outgoing_echo
15:     @sender_id = (@outgoing_echo ? @response.recipient_id : @response.sender_id)
16:     @message_type = (@outgoing_echo ? :outgoing : :incoming)
17:     @attachments = (@response.attachments || [])
18:   end
19: 
20:   def perform
21:     # This channel might require reauthorization, may be owner might have changed the fb password
22:     return if @inbox.channel.reauthorization_required?
23: 
24:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
25:       build_contact_inbox
26:       build_message
27:     end
28:   rescue Koala::Facebook::AuthenticationError => e
29:     Rails.logger.warn("Facebook authentication error for inbox: #{@inbox.id} with error: #{e.message}")
30:     Rails.logger.error e
31:     @inbox.channel.authorization_error!
32:   rescue StandardError => e
33:     ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: @inbox.account).capture_exception
34:     true
35:   end
36: 
37:   private
38: 
39:   def build_contact_inbox
40:     @contact_inbox = ::ContactInboxWithContactBuilder.new(
41:       source_id: @sender_id,
42:       inbox: @inbox,
43:       contact_attributes: contact_params
44:     ).perform
45:   end
46: 
47:   def build_message
48:     @message = conversation.messages.create!(message_params)
49: 
50:     @attachments.each do |attachment|
51:       process_attachment(attachment)
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def conversation
56:     @conversation ||= set_conversation_based_on_inbox_config
57:   end
58: 
59:   def set_conversation_based_on_inbox_config
60:     if @inbox.lock_to_single_conversation
61:       Conversation.where(conversation_params).order(created_at: :desc).first || build_conversation
62:     else
63:       find_or_build_for_multiple_conversations
64:     end
65:   end
66: 
67:   def find_or_build_for_multiple_conversations
68:     # If lock to single conversation is disabled, we will create a new conversation if previous conversation is resolved
69:     last_conversation = Conversation.where(conversation_params).where.not(status: :resolved).order(created_at: :desc).first
70:     return build_conversation if last_conversation.nil?
71: 
72:     last_conversation
73:   end
74: 
75:   def build_conversation
76:     Conversation.create!(conversation_params.merge(
77:                            contact_inbox_id: @contact_inbox.id
78:                          ))
79:   end
80: 
81:   def location_params(attachment)
82:     lat = attachment['payload']['coordinates']['lat']
83:     long = attachment['payload']['coordinates']['long']
84:     {
85:       external_url: attachment['url'],
86:       coordinates_lat: lat,
87:       coordinates_long: long,
88:       fallback_title: attachment['title']
89:     }
90:   end
91: 
92:   def fallback_params(attachment)
93:     {
94:       fallback_title: attachment['title'],
95:       external_url: attachment['url'] || attachment.dig('payload', 'url')
96:     }
97:   end
98: 
99:   # Facebook shared posts point to page URLs, not downloadable media URLs.
100:   # Keep this Facebook-only so Messenger/Instagram share attachments still use the parent media handling.
101:   def normalize_file_type(type)
102:     return :fallback if type.to_sym == :share
103: 
104:     super
105:   end
106: 
107:   def conversation_params
108:     {
109:       account_id: @inbox.account_id,
110:       inbox_id: @inbox.id,
111:       contact_id: @contact_inbox.contact_id
112:     }
113:   end
114: 
115:   def message_params
116:     content_attributes = {
117:       in_reply_to_external_id: response.in_reply_to_external_id
118:     }
119:     content_attributes[:external_echo] = true if @outgoing_echo
120: 
121:     {
122:       account_id: conversation.account_id,
123:       inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
124:       message_type: @message_type,
125:       status: @outgoing_echo ? :delivered : :sent,
126:       content: response.content,
127:       source_id: response.identifier,
128:       content_attributes: content_attributes,
129:       sender: @outgoing_echo ? nil : @contact_inbox.contact
130:     }
131:   end
132: 
133:   def process_contact_params_result(result)
134:     {
135:       name: "#{result['first_name'] || 'John'} #{result['last_name'] || 'Doe'}",
136:       account_id: @inbox.account_id,
137:       avatar_url: result['profile_pic']
138:     }
139:   end
140: 
141:   # rubocop:disable Metrics/AbcSize
142:   # rubocop:disable Metrics/MethodLength
143:   def contact_params
144:     begin
145:       k = Koala::Facebook::API.new(@inbox.channel.page_access_token) if @inbox.facebook?
146:       result = k.get_object(@sender_id) || {}
147:     rescue Koala::Facebook::AuthenticationError => e
148:       Rails.logger.warn("Facebook authentication error for inbox: #{@inbox.id} with error: #{e.message}")
149:       Rails.logger.error e
150:       @inbox.channel.authorization_error!
151:       raise
152:     rescue Koala::Facebook::ClientError => e
153:       result = {}
154:       # OAuthException, code: 100, error_subcode: 2018218, message: (#100) No profile available for this user
155:       # We don't need to capture this error as we don't care about contact params in case of echo messages
156:       if e.message.include?('2018218')
157:         Rails.logger.warn e
158:       else
159:         ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: @inbox.account).capture_exception unless @outgoing_echo
160:       end
161:     rescue StandardError => e
162:       result = {}
163:       ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: @inbox.account).capture_exception
164:     end
165:     process_contact_params_result(result)
166:   end
167:   # rubocop:enable Metrics/AbcSize
168:   # rubocop:enable Metrics/MethodLength
169: end

1: require 'net/imap'
2: 
3: class Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob < MutexApplicationJob
4:   queue_as :scheduled_jobs
5: 
6:   def perform(channel, interval = 1)
7:     return unless should_fetch_email?(channel)
8: 
9:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::EMAIL_MESSAGE_MUTEX, inbox_id: channel.inbox.id)
10: 
11:     with_lock(key, 5.minutes) do
12:       process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
13:     end
14:   rescue *ExceptionList::IMAP_EXCEPTIONS => e
15:     Rails.logger.error "Authorization error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
16:   rescue IOError, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, Net::IMAP::NoResponseError, Net::IMAP::BadResponseError, Net::IMAP::InvalidResponseError,
17:          Net::IMAP::ResponseParseError, Net::IMAP::ResponseReadError, Net::IMAP::ResponseTooLargeError => e
18:     Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
19:   rescue LockAcquisitionError
20:     Rails.logger.error "Lock failed for #{channel.inbox.id}"
21:   rescue StandardError => e
22:     ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
23:   end
24: 
25:   private
26: 
27:   def should_fetch_email?(channel)
28:     channel.imap_enabled? && !channel.reauthorization_required?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
32:     inbound_emails = if channel.microsoft?
33:                        Imap::MicrosoftFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
34:                      elsif channel.google?
35:                        Imap::GoogleFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
36:                      else
37:                        Imap::FetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
38:                      end
39: 
40:     inbound_emails.each do |inbound_mail|
41:       process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
42:     end
43:   rescue OAuth2::Error => e
44:     Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
45:     channel.authorization_error!
46:   end
47: 
48:   def should_skip_email?(message_id)
49:     failure_count = Rails.cache.read("email_failures:#{message_id}") || 0
50:     failure_count >= 3
51:   end
52: 
53:   def mark_email_as_failed(message_id)
54:     failure_count = Rails.cache.read("email_failures:#{message_id}") || 0
55:     Rails.cache.write("email_failures:#{message_id}", failure_count + 1, expires_in: 6.hours)
56:   end
57: 
58:   def process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
59:     # Skip if this email has failed multiple times recently
60:     if should_skip_email?(inbound_mail.message_id)
61:       Rails.logger.warn "[IMAP] Skipping problematic email: #{inbound_mail.message_id}"
62:       return
63:     end
64: 
65:     begin
66:       Timeout.timeout(email_processing_timeout) do
67:         Imap::ImapMailbox.new.process(inbound_mail, channel)
68:       end
69:     rescue Timeout::Error
70:       mark_email_as_failed(inbound_mail.message_id)
71:       Rails.logger.error "[IMAP] Email processing timeout (#{email_processing_timeout}s): #{inbound_mail.message_id}"
72:     rescue StandardError => e
73:       mark_email_as_failed(inbound_mail.message_id)
74:       Rails.logger.error "[IMAP] Failed to process email #{inbound_mail.message_id}: #{e.message}"
75:       ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
76:     end
77:   end
78: 
79:   def email_processing_timeout
80:     GlobalConfigService.load('EMAIL_PROCESSING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS', 60).to_i
81:   end
82: end
